hook.text: In Europe, 27 countries abolished border checks. You can drive from Lisbon to Helsinki freely.

buildup.text: The Schengen Agreement, signed in 1985, created the world's largest free-travel zone.

discovery.text: Over 400 million people move freely across borders that once required passports and visas.

twist.text: The 2015 refugee crisis and COVID-19 saw countries temporarily restore border checks.

climax.text: Open borders work until a crisis tests them—then the fences come right back.

punchline.text: Borders can vanish—but they never really forget how to return.

quiz.question: What does the Schengen Agreement allow?
options: Free movement across 27 European countries without border checks
Free trade but not free movement
